question.text: Speculative losses from derivatives can be set off against:

answer.text: Only speculative business income; they cannot be set off against non-speculative income or salary/capital gains
Any business income, whether speculative or non-speculative
All types of income without restriction

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under the Income Tax Act, speculative losses can only be set off against speculative income. They cannot be adjusted against non-speculative business income, salary, capital gains, or any other head of income. Unabsorbed speculative losses can be carried forward for 4 assessment years and set off only against speculative income.
**The Trap:** Speculative losses have LIMITED set-off ability. This is a significant tax disadvantage of derivative trading for individuals.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The restriction on set-off of speculative losses is a commonly tested tax concept.
**Key Takeaway:** Speculative loss set-off: only against speculative income. Carry forward: 4 years. Cannot set off against salary, capital gains, or non-speculative income.
